name: Electroweak baryogenesis
summary: Generates baryon asymmetry during a strong first-order electroweak phase transition: bubble walls separate broken and unbroken phases; CP-violating reflection of particles off the walls plus EW sphaleron processes in the unbroken phase produce a net baryon number that is preserved in the broken phase. Requires extension of the SM scalar sector since the SM Higgs gives only a crossover.

addresses_puzzles.note: Requires both a strongly first-order EWPT (extended scalar sector: 2HDM, MSSM with light stop, singlet extensions) and additional CP violation beyond CKM. Current LHC constraints have squeezed but not closed the parameter space.

touches_benchmarks.note: New CP-violating phases generate electron and quark EDMs at one or two loops; viable EWBG parameter space is sharply constrained by JILA / ACME bounds.
Singlet extensions generate Higgs portal mixing; couplings deviate from SM at the few-percent level.
Strong first-order PT generates a stochastic GW background at mHz-Hz frequencies; LISA / DECIGO targets.

new_fields: extended scalar sector (2HDM, singlet, MSSM stop)
introduces.note: Scalar potential parameters + new CP-violating phases.

history: Sakharov conditions (1967); Kuzmin-Rubakov-Shaposhnikov sphaleron analysis (1985). Pure SM EWBG ruled out by m_H > 75 GeV (no first-order PT). Active research: 2HDM-EWBG, MSSM-EWBG (largely killed by LHC), singlet-extended EWBG.

references.citation: Kuzmin, Rubakov, Shaposhnikov, 'On the anomalous electroweak baryon-number nonconservation in the early universe', Phys. Lett. B 155, 36 (1985)
Morrissey and Ramsey-Musolf, 'Electroweak baryogenesis', New J. Phys. 14, 125003 (2012)
